name: Roast Beef in the Clay Pot
recipeInstructions:
"Roast beef steamed in the clay pot offers a unique texture not found with other cooking methods. However, it will cook much faster than you expect; rely on your meat thermometer for accuracy. The size of both the pot and roast will affect cooking time. For rare beef, check the thermometer after 40 minutes. You may omit vegetables if you prefer an individual dish."
1. Soak your largest clay pot, top and bottom, in water for fifteen minutes.
2. Trim excess fat from the beef and season with salt and pepper.
3. Place the seasoned beef into the pot and insert a meat thermometer.
4. Add all other ingredients to the pot, including 1 teaspoon of salt and additional pepper.
5. Cover the pot and place it in a cold oven.
6. Set the oven temperature to 480 degrees Fahrenheit.
7. For rare beef, check the meat thermometer after 40 minutes; for medium, check after 60 minutes; for well-done, check after 90 minutes.
8. Remove the pot from the oven when the thermometer is nearly at the desired temperature (do not wait until it reads exactly).
9. Pour the liquid into a saucepan and bring to a boil. Thicken with about half a teaspoon of arrowroot.
This recipe strictly adheres to the original instructions, maintaining clarity and consistency while ensuring safety and accuracy in the cooking process.
